title: रामरक्षा स्तोत्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री रामरक्षा स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
ॐ अस्य श्रीरामरक्षास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य।
बुधकौशिक ऋषिः। श्रीसीतारामचन्द्रो देवता।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। सीता शक्तिः।
श्रीमद्धनुमान् कीलकम्।
श्रीसीतारामचन्द्रप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
ध्यायेदाजानुबाहुं धृतशरधनुषं बद्धपद्मासनस्थं
पीतं वासो वसानं नवकमलदलस्पर्धिनेत्रं प्रसन्नम्।
वामाङ्कारूढसीतामुखकमलमिलल्लोचनं नीरदाभं
नानालङ्कारदीप्तं दधतमुरुजटामण्डलं रामचन्द्रम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
चरितं रघुनाथस्य शतकोटिप्रविस्तरम्।
एकैकमक्षरं पुंसां महापातकनाशनम्॥१॥

ध्यात्वा नीलोत्पलश्यामं रामं राजीवलोचनम्।
जानकीलक्ष्मणोपेतं जटामुकुटमण्डितम्॥२॥

सासितूणधनुर्बाणपाणिं नक्तञ्चरान्तकम्।
स्वलीलया जगत्त्रातुमाविर्भूतमजं विभुम्॥३॥

रामो राजमणिः सदा विजयते रामं रमेशं भजे
रामेणाभिहता निशाचरचमू रामाय तस्मै नमः।
रामान्नास्ति परायणं परतरं रामस्य दासोऽस्म्यहं
रामे चित्तलयः सदा भवतु मे भो राम मामुद्धर॥४॥

रामलक्ष्मणसीताभ्यो नमः।

राम इत्यक्षरद्वन्द्वं सर्वमन्त्रोपरि स्थितम्।
तारयत्यखिलं लोकं राम इत्यक्षरद्वयम्॥५॥

रामाय रामभद्राय रामचन्द्राय वेधसे।
रघुनाथाय नाथाय सीतायाः पतये नमः॥६॥

श्रीराम राम रघुनन्दन राम राम
श्रीराम राम भरताग्रज राम राम।
श्रीराम राम रणकर्कश राम राम
श्रीराम राम शरणं भव राम राम॥७॥

श्रीरामचन्द्रचरणौ मनसा स्मरामि
श्रीरामचन्द्रचरणौ वचसा गृणामि।
श्रीरामचन्द्रचरणौ शिरसा नमामि
श्रीरामचन्द्रचरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये॥८॥

मातां रामो मत्पिता रामचन्द्रः
स्वामी रामो मत्सखा रामचन्द्रः।
सर्वस्वं मे रामचन्द्रो दयालुर्-
नान्यं जाने नैव जाने न जाने॥९॥

दक्षिणे लक्ष्मणो यस्य वामे तु जनकात्मजा।
पुरतो मारुतिर्यस्य तं वन्दे रघुनन्दनम्॥१०॥

लोकाभिरामं रणरङ्गधीरं
राजीवनेत्रं रघुवंशनाथम्।
कारुण्यरूपं करुणाकरं तं
श्रीरामचन्द्रं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥११॥

मनोजवं मारुततुल्यवेगं
जितेन्द्रियं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम्।
वातात्मजं वानरयूथमुख्यं
श्रीरामदूतं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥१२॥

कूजन्तं रामरामेति मधुरं मधुराक्षरम्।
आरुह्य कविताशाखां वन्दे वाल्मीकिकोकिलम्॥१३॥

आपदामपहर्तारं दातारं सर्वसम्पदाम्।
लोकाभिरामं श्रीरामं भूयो भूयो नमाम्यहम्॥१४॥

भर्जनं भवबीजानामर्जनं सुखसम्पदाम्।
तर्जनं यमदूतानां रामरामेति गर्जनम्॥१५॥

रामो राजमणिः सदा विजयते रामं रमेशं भजे
रामेणाभिहता निशाचरचमू रामाय तस्मै नमः।
रामान्नास्ति परायणं परतरं रामस्य दासोऽस्म्यहं
रामे चित्तलयः सदा भवतु मे भो राम मामुद्धर॥१६॥

श्रीराम रामेति रामेति रमे रामे मनोरमे।
सहस्रनाम तत्तुल्यं रामनाम वरानने॥१७॥

॥ इति श्रीबुधकौशिकविरचितं श्रीरामरक्षास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ramaraksha Stotram ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Om Asya Shri Ramaraksha Stotra Mantrasya.
Budhakaushika Rishih. Shri Sitaramachandro Devata.
Anushtup Chhandah. Sita Shaktih.
Shrimaddhanuman Kilakam.
Shri Sitaramachandra Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Dhyayedajanubahum Dhritasharadhanusham Baddhapadmasanastham
Peetam Vaso Vasanam Navakamadalaspardhinetram Prasannam.
VamankaarudhasitamukhakamalaMillalllochanam Neeradabham
Nanalankaaradiptam Dadhatamurujataamandalam Ramachandram.

|| Stotram ||
Charitam Raghunatasya Shatakotipravistaram.
Ekaikamaksharam Pumsam Mahapatakanaashanam.||1||

Dhyatva Neelotpalashyamam Ramam Rajeevalocanam.
Janakilakshmanopetam Jatamukutamanditam.||2||

Sasitoonadhanurbanapaanim Naktancharantakam.
Svalilaya Jagattraatumavirbhootamajam Vibhum.||3||

Ramo Rajamanih Sada Vijayate Ramam Ramesham Bhaje
Ramenabhihata Nishacharachamu Ramaya Tasmai Namah.
Ramannasti Parayanam Parataram Ramasya Dasosmyaham
Rame Chittalayah Sada Bhavatu Me Bho Rama Mamuddhara.||4||

Ramalakshmanasetabhyo Namah.

Rama Ityaksharadvandvam Sarvamantrropari Sthitam.
Tarayatyakhilam Lokam Rama Ityaksharadvayam.||5||

Ramaya Ramabhadraya Ramachandraya Vedhase.
Raghunathaya Nathaya Sitayah Pataye Namah.||6||

Shri Rama Rama Raghunandana Rama Rama
Shri Rama Rama Bharatagraja Rama Rama.
Shri Rama Rama Ranakarkasha Rama Rama
Shri Rama Rama Sharanam Bhava Rama Rama.||7||

Shri Ramachandracharanau Manasa Smarami
Shri Ramachandracharanau Vachasa Grinami.
Shri Ramachandracharanau Shirasa Namami
Shri Ramachandracharanau Sharanam Prapadye.||8||

Mataam Ramo Matpita Ramachandrah
Svami Ramo Matsakha Ramachandrah.
Sarvasvam Me Ramachandro Dayalur-
Nanyam Jane Naiva Jane Na Jane.||9||

Dakshine Lakshmano Yasya Vame Tu Janakatmaja.
Purato Marutiryasya Tam Vande Raghunandanam.||10||

Lokabhiramam Ranarangadheeram
Rajivanetram Raghuvansha Natham.
Karunyarupam Karunakaram Tam
Shri Ramachandram Sharanam Prapadye.||11||

Manojavm Marutatulyavegam
Jitendriyam Buddhimatam Varishtham.
Vatatmajam Vanarayuthamukhyam
Shri Ramadutam Sharanam Prapadye.||12||

Koojantam Ramaraameti Madhuram Madhuraaksharam.
Aruhya Kavitashakham Vande Valmikikokilam.||13||

Aapadamapahartaram Dataram Sarvasampadam.
Lokabhiramam Shriramam Bhuyo Bhuyo Namamyaham.||14||

Bharjanam Bhavabeejanaam Arjanam Sukhasampadam.
Tarjanam Yamadutanam Ramaraameti Garjanam.||15||

Ramo Rajamanih Sada Vijayate Ramam Ramesham Bhaje
Ramenabhihata Nishacharachamu Ramaya Tasmai Namah.
Ramannasti Parayanam Parataram Ramasya Dasosmyaham
Rame Chittalayah Sada Bhavatu Me Bho Rama Mamuddhara.||16||

Shri Rama Rameti Rameti Rame Rame Manorame.
Sahasranama Tattulyam Ramanama Varanane.||17||

|| Iti Shri Budhakaushika Virachitam Shri Ramaraksha Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: रामरक्षा स्तोत्र एक अत्यंत प्रभावशाली रक्षा कवच है। इसमें कहा गया है कि रघुनाथ का चरित्र सौ करोड़ विस्तार वाला है और इसका एक-एक अक्षर महापातकों का नाश करने वाला है। नीलकमल के समान श्यामवर्ण, कमलनयन, जानकी-लक्ष्मण सहित, जटामुकुटधारी श्रीराम का ध्यान करना चाहिए। राम राजमणि हैं, सदा विजयी हैं। राम नाम के दो अक्षर सम्पूर्ण लोक का उद्धार करते हैं। राम ही माता हैं, पिता हैं, स्वामी हैं, सखा हैं - राम ही सर्वस्व हैं। राम नाम का गर्जन भव-बीजों का दहन, सुख-सम्पदा का अर्जन और यमदूतों का तर्जन है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीरामरक्षास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य बुधकौशिक ऋषिः। श्रीसीतारामचन्द्रो देवता। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। सीता शक्तिः। श्रीमद्धनुमान् कीलकम्। श्रीसीतारामचन्द्रप्रीत्यर्थे रामरक्षास्तोत्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish a divine protective shield around the devotee
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins including great sins (mahapatakas)
Invokes victory in all endeavors and competitions
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of death and yamadutas
Invokes the blessing of all types of wealth and prosperity
Provides protection from enemies and evil forces
Invokes the supreme grace of lord rama
description: The Ramraksha Stotra, known in Sanskrit as रामरक्षा स्तोत्र, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Rama. This revered text originates from Budhakaushika Rishi - Traditional Sanskrit. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove fear of death and Yamadutas as well as grant victory in all endeavors and competitions. Additionally, it is known to destroy all sins including great sins (mahapatakas). It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ram Navami for maximum spiritual benefit. With 17 verses and a reading time of about 15 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ramraksha Stotra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ramraksha Stotra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. A powerful protective prayer to Lord Rama by Rishi Budhakaushika.

Ram Raksha Stotra benefits chanting daily
Ram Raksha Stotra 38 verses anga-nyasa kavacha

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Ramraksha Stotra and under what circumstances?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ramraksha Stotra is attributed to Sage Budha Kaushika, identified by Hindu tradition with Brahmarishi Vishvamitra. The composition account preserved by the Gita Press and other commentators holds that Lord Shiva himself dictated the entire 38-verse hymn to the rishi in a dream during the night, and that Budha Kaushika wrote the verses down at dawn from his memory of the dream-revelation. This direct Shaiva-Vaishnava transmission is one reason the stotra is regarded by both Smarta and Vaishnava traditions as exceptionally efficacious. The stotra is preserved in chapter 73 of the Padma Purana as the Ramaraksha section. (Sources: Wikipedia Rama Raksha Stotra; Wisdom Library Padma Purana ch. 73; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Ramraksha Stotra structurally organised across its 38 verses?
seoFaqs.answer: The 38 verses follow the classical kavacha or armor structure used in protective stotras. The opening verses establish the seer (Vishvamitra), deity (Sri Rama), metre (anushtup) and viniyoga or application. The middle verses constitute the anga-nyasa: each named limb of the devotee from the head (Raghava-rakshatu shirasi) to the feet (charanau Sita-pati) is consigned to the protection of a different name of Rama, creating a complete protective shell. The final verses include the Rama Naam Mahima passages, the famous Ramo Rajamani sada vijayate, and the phalashruti promising the merit of one thousand Vishnu-namas for a single utterance of Rama-naam. (Sources: Wikipedia; Hindupedia Sri Rama Raksha Stotram; Stotra Ratnavali.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the verse Ramo Rajamani sada vijayate?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse number twenty-six of the Ramraksha Stotra, Ramo Rajamani sada vijayate, is a one-line mantra that names Rama through eight epithets and asserts his eternal victory: Rama is the king-jewel who is ever victorious, the king of kings, the master of kings, the abode of bliss, the path of bliss, the embodied Rama, devoted to Rama, repeated as Rama Rama. It is regarded as a complete eight-syllable Vijaya-mantra in itself. Devotees who cannot chant the entire stotra often recite this single verse 21, 108 or 1008 times for victory in court cases, examinations, business deals or any contested situation. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sadhana Sansar Ram Raksha Stotra; Stotra Ratnavali.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the recommended frequencies and times for chanting the Ramraksha Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional pandits prescribe a graduated practice: beginners chant once daily after morning bath, intermediate practitioners chant three or seven times daily, and advanced devotees recite eleven times to maintain protective effect throughout twenty-four hours. Pradosha-kala twilight, Brahma muhurta before sunrise, and Tuesday and Saturday evenings dedicated to Hanuman are considered the most potent windows. Special parayana of the entire stotra is performed during the nine days of Rama Navami in Chaitra, on Hanuman Jayanti, during Sundara Kanda parayana weeks, and as a 21-day or 40-day anushthana when facing serious health, legal or relationship challenges. (Sources: Vedic Rishi; Sadhana Sansar; Astrosage.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Ramraksha Stotra differ from a Rama Sahasranama or a Hanuman Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The three texts serve different ritual functions in the Rama-bhakti tradition. The Hanuman Chalisa, composed by Tulsidas in Awadhi, is a 40-verse devotional poem to Hanuman emphasising surrender and removal of obstacles; it is used for invocation and bhakti-bhava. The Rama Sahasranama, found in the Padma Purana, contemplates Rama through 1000 names and is a meditation tool emphasising knowledge of his all-pervasive nature; it is used for jnana-yoga and Sahasranama-archana. The Ramraksha Stotra is specifically a protective kavacha that invokes Rama as the protector of every named bodily limb and life-circumstance; it is used for raksha or armor function. Many households recite all three in sequence during their morning sandhya. (Sources: Wikipedia Rama Raksha Stotra; Hindupedia; Vedic Rishi.)

seoFaqs.question: Are there particular days or events when the Ramraksha Stotra should not be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotra has no traditionally prohibited day; it is among the few protective hymns recommended for unrestricted recitation including during ashaucha or family impurity periods. However, ritual purists advise reciting it mentally rather than aloud during meals, while in the toilet, during the immediate moments of menstruation onset, and within twenty-four hours of a birth or death in the family. Some commentators add that during a solar or lunar eclipse the stotra should be chanted continuously rather than once and stopped, since the protective function should not be interrupted while the eclipse-shadow influences are active. There is also a custom of pausing recitation during the exact muhurta of a Rahu Kalam-overlap on Saturdays and resuming after. (Sources: Sadhana Sansar; Vedic Rishi; Astrosage.)
